title: erigeron aliceae
intro: Erigeron aliceae, also known as the Alice's fleabane, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves and flowers. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections, providing health benefits such as pain relief and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Erigeron aliceae are commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
